feedingProfile.preferredNpk: balanced (e.g. 10-10-10), avoid high nitrogen which reduces blooms

commonIssues: Tender summer annual; killed by frost and not winter-hardy outside USDA zones where frost occurs. Treat as a single-season planting.
Susceptible to powdery mildew, Botrytis, leaf spot and root rots, especially in wet or poorly drained conditions.
Watch for spider mites and thrips, particularly in hot, dry weather.
Overwatering or poorly drained soil causes root rot; let the top 1-2 inches of soil dry between waterings.
Regular deadheading keeps plants blooming from early summer until frost.
Cell sap plus sunlight can cause contact dermatitis on broken skin.

source.citation: NC State Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox - Tagetes patula (French Marigold): full sun 6+ hours of direct sun (partial shade 2-6 hours); average, well-drained, evenly moist soil, acid to neutral pH below 8.0; summer annual, not frost-hardy; 6-12 in tall, 6-18 in wide, clumping bushy habit, medium growth rate, low maintenance; excellent for containers, patios and edging; blooms spring into fall with deadheading.

NC State Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox - Tagetes patula: susceptible to powdery mildew, Botrytis, leaf spot and root rots; spider mites and thrips may infest plants; contact dermatitis possible from cell sap plus sunlight on broken skin.
NC State Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox - Tagetes patula: summer annual, not frost-hardy (frost sensitive).

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder - Tagetes patula (French Marigold): water requirement Medium; full sun, with some light afternoon shade in hot summer climates; bloom time June to frost; height 0.50-1.00 ft (6-12 in), spread 0.50-0.75 ft; annual; low maintenance; suggested use beds, edgings, containers, good cut flowers; susceptible to powdery mildew, Botrytis, leaf spot and rots, spider mites and thrips.
Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder - Tagetes patula: bloom time stated as 'June to frost', supporting productive months June-October in temperate growing regions.

The Old Farmer's Almanac - How to Plant and Grow Marigolds: provide about 1 inch of water per week and let the soil dry out a bit between waterings; do not overwater (excess moisture can cause root rot); sow outdoors after the last frost or start indoors 6-8 weeks prior; French marigolds stay under 2 feet and are well suited to containers. (Page returns HTTP 403 to automated fetch; values taken from indexed search snippet, 2026-06-02.)

Container sizing for French marigold: dwarf French marigolds grow well in a 6-inch (approx 2-3 L) pot; a 10-12 inch pot (approx 5-11 L) holds several plants. The container minimums here reflect a single compact French marigold plant; values estimated from horticultural guidance rather than a single .edu container-volume citation.

Confidence note: baseIntervalDays (3) is derived from extension guidance to water when the top 1-2 inches of soil dry and to supply ~1 inch/week, not a directly published interval; hotWeatherMultiplier, rainSkipThresholdMm, feeding interval, container liters, and temperature thresholds (coldHardyMinC, heatStressC) are conservative estimates converted from qualitative extension guidance (full sun, medium water, frost-sensitive annual). No single authoritative source publishes these exact numeric values.
